message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
note: Adding batch_size param to multiple options (Option class + processing logic). Need core.py and types.py.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
note: My feature (Dynamic Default Resolution for Click Options) needs to modify Option class default handling and help text formatting in core.py, plus type conversion logic in types.py. What files does your feature need? Let's find a disjoint split - if we both need core.py, we may need to coordinate on specific functions/sections within it.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/click/core.py
your_files: src/click/types.py
note: I don't actually need types.py - my feature (dynamic dict-based defaults) only touches core.py: Option.__init__ (validate dict default keys/values), Option.get_help_extra (add '[default: (dynamic)]' text), and Command.main (add a second-pass resolution loop after args/envvar/config parsing, before validation). You can have types.py fully. Since we BOTH need core.py, let's avoid clobbering by keeping edits scoped: I will ONLY touch Option.__init__, Option.get_help_extra, and Command.main (adding a new small private helper if needed). Please tell me which specific methods/regions in core.py your batch_size feature touches so we can confirm zero overlap. If your changes are in different methods than mine, we should be safe to both edit core.py in parallel.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/click/core.py
your_files: src/click/types.py,src/click/core.py
note: My batch_size feature is fully containable in the Option class in core.py: (1) Option.__init__ (~line 2533) - add a new 'batch_size' keyword-only param appended after existing params, with validation (TypeError if set without multiple=True, ValueError if negative); (2) a new dedicated method/override (e.g. type_cast_value or process_value) to chunk multiple values into batches; (3) Option.to_info_dict (~line 2665) - add one 'batch_size' key. I do NOT need types.py at all - it's yours entirely. For core.py, I'll only touch those 3 isolated spots in the Option class and won't touch get_default/get_help_record/default-resolution logic - that's yours. Since we're both in Option.__init__, please add your params in a separate block from mine (I'll append batch_size as the last new param) to minimize overlap. OK with you?

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/click/core.py
your_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
note: Agreed. I'll only touch: Option.__init__ (my own dynamic-default validation block, separate from your batch_size param), Option.get_help_extra (default text), and Command.main (second-pass resolution loop). I will not touch to_info_dict, type_cast_value/process_value, or anywhere related to batch_size. You own types.py entirely and the batch_size-specific spots in core.py. Proceeding now.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/click/core.py
your_files: src/click/types.py
note: Confirmed zero overlap: my batch_size feature touches Option.__init__ (appending 'batch_size' as the final new kwarg, separate block from your dict-default validation), a new method for batching (type_cast_value/process_value override - not touched by you), and Option.to_info_dict (adding one 'batch_size' key - separate line from your default-related info). I won't touch get_help_extra or Command.main. Accepting split: I own the batch_size additions in core.py, you own dynamic-default additions in core.py + types.py is fully mine (I won't touch it, but you said you don't need it either). Proceeding to implement now.

type: DONE
my_files: src/click/core.py
note: Finished implementing batch_size: added Option.__init__ param (after 'multiple', before 'count'), validation (TypeError if used without multiple, ValueError if <=0), self.batch_size attribute, to_info_dict entry, and a new Option.type_cast_value override that chunks the flat tuple into batches. Did not touch get_default, get_help_extra, or Command.main. types.py left untouched - fully yours. Diff written to patch.txt.

type: DONE
my_files: src/click/core.py
note: Finished implementing dynamic default resolution: (1) Option.__init__ validates dict-based default specs right after super().__init__ (TypeError for non-string keys / non-dict values), (2) Option.get_default returns None for dict defaults so type casting doesn't choke on the unresolved dict, (3) Option.get_help_extra shows '[default: (dynamic)]' when show_default is set, (4) added Command._resolve_dynamic_defaults(ctx), called from Command.parse_args right after the main param loop - it resolves dict defaults against other options' final values (matched by '--opt-name' in p.opts/secondary_opts), only when the target's ParameterSource is DEFAULT, with later dict entries taking precedence. All 739 existing tests pass, verified basic/multi-dep/envvar/help-text/validation scenarios manually. Did not touch to_info_dict, type_cast_value, or process_value. types.py untouched. Diff written to patch.txt in my own environment.
